extraction.one_line_summary: CORT Magnus won from a breakaway that he drove himself, holding off a surging ROGLIC Primoz by meters after 2km climb finish.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIC Primoz targeted time bonuses at 2km steep finish, taking second for GC advantage rather than committing early attack.
BikeExchange set tempo after missing break despite no GC interest, signaling changed team dynamics without traditional sprint leadout focus.
Crosswinds on bridge approach shattered ELLISSONDE Kenny's defense before climb even started, exposing lightweight climber vulnerability on flat coastal roads.
MOVISTAR placed VALVERDE Alejandro for podium finish without chasing own breakaway rider, showing calculated stage-vs-GC resource allocation.

rider_signals.CORT Magnus: positive: drove breakaway all day then held off charging ROGLIC on steep 2km finish, exceptional diesel-then-punch combination
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: reclaimed red jersey with calculated sprint for second place and time bonuses, avoided premature jersey defense
rider_signals.ELLISSONDE Kenny: negative: lost red jersey before climb started due to crosswind energy hemorrhage at 114lbs, weight liability on exposed flat coastal roads
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: neutral: close second to ROGLIC on finish climb but MOVISTAR showed they'll prioritize his placement over chasing stages
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: negative: BikeExchange setting tempo suggests desperation tactics, MATTHEWS couldn't close final 500m gap after team's flat chase work
